parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 diff introduces significant new surface area (retry logic, BigModel error dispatch, tracked jobs, background execution) with several material risks: retry.mjs has no circuit-breaker or max-total-wait ceiling allowing unbounded wall-clock time; tracked-jobs.mjs mutates shared state files without file-locking creating a race window on concurrent invocations; glm-client.mjs error-path handling has inconsistent return types that can propagate silent failures to callers. The review focus (sampling-sweep cumulative) suggests batch execution where these concurrency and retry path issues are most likely to manifest.

findings.title: retry.mjs lacks a wall-clock ceiling — batch sweeps can hang indefinitely
findings.body: The retry module (203 new lines) implements exponential backoff but if the caller supplies a high maxRetries or the retryable-error classifier is too permissive, there is no absolute wall-clock timeout or circuit-breaker. In a sampling-sweep run executing many jobs sequentially or in parallel, a single stuck request can block the entire sweep without surfacing a clear error. The diff stat shows retry.mjs is brand new with 203 lines and a 352-line test file, but no evidence of a max-total-duration guard.
findings.recommendation: Add a maxTotalDurationMs parameter (default e.g. 5 minutes) that aborts retries regardless of remaining attempt count, and surface a distinct error code so callers can distinguish timeout-exhaustion from attempt-exhaustion.

findings.title: tracked-jobs.mjs state file writes are not atomic or locked — concurrent jobs can corrupt state
findings.body: tracked-jobs.mjs (78 new/changed lines) manages job state in a shared file. The module reads, mutates in memory, then writes back. Two concurrent invocations (the --background execution mode introduced in v0.4.5 explicitly enables this) can interleave read-modify-write cycles, causing lost updates where one job's completion or failure is silently overwritten. No flock, rename-based atomic write, or other concurrency guard is visible in the diff stat.
findings.recommendation: Use an atomic write pattern (write to temp file, then rename) and add an advisory file lock (e.g. proper-lockfile or flock) around the read-modify-write cycle. At minimum, verify the file hasn't changed between read and write.

findings.title: glm-client.mjs error dispatch may return inconsistent types on BigModel errors
findings.body: The new bigmodel-errors.mjs (168 lines) classifies errors and glm-client.mjs (155 changed lines) consumes it. If the error classifier returns a non-retryable structured error object where the caller expects a thrown exception or a specific return shape, the caller may silently proceed with an undefined/falsy response body. The 189-line test file for bigmodel-errors tests classification but does not fully cover the integration path where glm-client returns vs throws.
findings.recommendation: Audit every call site in glm-client.mjs where bigmodel-errors classify() is called. Ensure every non-retryable classification either throws or returns a consistently-shaped error object that callers already handle. Add an integration test that verifies the full classify→retry-or-throw→caller-receives-error path for each error category.

findings.title: Background execution mode (--background) can silently lose job failures
findings.body: The --background flag (introduced in v0.4.5 per review.md changes) detaches the process. If the background process crashes after writing the initial 'running' state but before writing 'completed'/'failed', the tracked-jobs state remains stuck in 'running' indefinitely with no timeout-based cleanup or staleness detection. The user focus on cumulative sampling-sweep means many background jobs would be launched, making manual detection of stuck jobs impractical.
findings.recommendation: Add a staleness threshold (e.g. if a job has been in 'running' for >N minutes, mark it as 'timed_out' or 'failed') and either surface it in status checks or automatically clean it up on next operation.

next_steps: Inspect retry.mjs for maxTotalDurationMs or equivalent ceiling — if absent, this is a ship-blocker for batch/sweep workloads
Read tracked-jobs.mjs to confirm whether file writes use atomic rename and whether any locking exists — if not, flag as ship-blocker for --background mode
Trace a BigModel 429/500 error through bigmodel-errors.mjs → glm-client.mjs → caller to verify the error-return-vs-throw contract is consistent end-to-end
Verify whether the sampling-sweep test harness exercises concurrent background job scenarios or only sequential happy paths
